titel: Commissioner Tzitzikostas delivered a speech at the event titled "Prague–Berlin–Copenhagen: The first EU pilot project for new international railway connections."
html:
European Commission Speech in Berlin, June 26, 2026

Ladies and gentlemen,

It is a great pleasure to be here with you in Berlin on this historic day for European railways. Today we celebrate...
tldr: The EU is launching a new project to improve train services between the cities of Prague, Berlin, and Copenhagen. The goal is to make it easier and more convenient to travel by train between these countries.
lattlast:
The European Commission, the body responsible for governing the EU's common policies, is launching a new pilot project focused on railways. This project concentrates on the route connecting the cities of Prague, Berlin, and Copenhagen to improve international train connections.

The aim of the project is to enhance cross-border travel within Europe by train. By investing in these specific routes, the goal is to make rail travel a more attractive and convenient option for travelers.

The announcement was made on June 26, 2026, in Berlin, marking the start of this initiative. This day is considered a significant milestone in the development of railway transport within Europe.
